config.title: Configuration
config.subtitle: Manage roles, permissions, and display settings for your workspace.
config.adminOnly: Only admins can change this setting
config.addWeightTooltip: Add a weight label with values 1..5 quickly
config.labelKeyInvalid: Key "{{key}}" must start with a letter and contain only letters, digits, and underscores.
config.keyHint: Letters, digits, underscore. Must start with a letter.
config.weightHint: Drives the weight picker on rule authoring pages.
config.builtinKeyHint: Built-in key. The name is locked, but its values and colors can be changed.
config.descriptionPlaceholder: What this label captures (e.g. Owning team)
config.noValuesHint: No values — authors will toggle this label as a boolean tag (true/false).
config.importRulesDescription: Bulk-import rules from a DQX YAML file or generate them from a data contract.
config.aiSettingsDescription: AI-assisted rule authoring and the rule suggester stay off until a serving endpoint is configured here.
config.aiSettingsEnabledHint: Turns on Build-with-AI, per-field suggestions, and the rule-mapping suggester app-wide.
config.aiSettingsRateLimitLabel: Rate limit (requests per user per hour)
config.aiSettingsVectorSearchDescription: Configure these to enable AI rule suggestions on monitored tables. Leave blank to keep the suggester off.
config.aiSettingsAdminOnlyHint: Only admins can change this setting.

discovery.title: Discovery
discovery.subtitle: Browse Unity Catalog assets to find tables for your data quality checks.

profiler.title: Profile & generate
profiler.subtitle: Profile tables to generate data quality rule suggestions based on data distribution.
profiler.newRunDescription: Select one or more tables (or an entire schema), configure sampling, and run the profiler.
profiler.rowFilterHint: Optional SQL condition applied before profiling.
profiler.toggleColumnsHint: Click to toggle. No selection = profile all columns.
profiler.removeOutliersHint: Exclude statistical outliers when computing min/max range checks.
profiler.outlierThresholdHint: Standard deviations from mean to consider an outlier (default: 3).
profiler.llmPkHint: Use AI to detect primary key columns and generate uniqueness checks.
profiler.noRuns: No profiling runs yet. Run the profiler above to get started.
profiler.submittedWaiting: Profiling job submitted — waiting for results...
profiler.permissionHint: Ask your workspace admin to grant the required Unity Catalog permissions, then try again.
profiler.permissionHintBatch: Ask your workspace admin to grant the required Unity Catalog permissions on the selected tables, then try again.
profiler.permissionHintTable: Ask your workspace admin to grant the required Unity Catalog permissions on this table.
profiler.moreFailed: … and {{count}} more table(s) failed to submit
profiler.noRulesGenerated: No rules were generated from the profiling data.

columnStats.title: Column statistics ({{count}})
columnStats.footnote: Stats are computed by the DQX profiler on the sampled subset of rows — use the Run history → View dialog to compare against past runs.

rulesActive.title: Active rules
rulesActive.subtitle: Approved rules currently enforced on your tables.
rulesActive.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this rule for
rulesActive.noColumnRules: No column / dataset rules found. Switch to "Cross-table" to view query-based rules.
rulesActive.noCrossTableHint: Cross-table SQL rules will appear here once created and approved.
rulesActive.noActiveRulesAuthor: Create and approve rules to see them here. Active rules are enforced during quality runs.
rulesActive.noActiveRulesViewer: No rules have been approved yet. Check Drafts & review for pending rule sets.

rulesCreate.title: Create Rules
rulesCreate.subtitle: Choose how you want to create data quality rules.
rulesCreate.singleTableDescription: Define column-level, schema-validation, and reference-table checks (e.g. foreign_key) for individual tables, with optional AI assistance.
rulesCreate.crossTableDescription: Write SQL queries that validate data across multiple tables or run dataset-level aggregation checks.
rulesCreate.profileDescription: Profile your data to auto-generate quality rules based on column statistics.
rulesCreate.importDescription: Bulk-import from a DQX YAML file or generate rules from an ODCS v3.x data contract.
rulesCreate.fromContractDescription: Generate rules from an ODCS v3.x data contract — schema, constraints, and explicit DQX checks.
rulesCreate.schemaDescription: Catch drift in columns, order, and types with a has_valid_schema rule — strict match or compatibility check.

rulesFromContract.title: Generate rules from data contract
rulesFromContract.subtitle: Upload an Open Data Contract Standard (ODCS) v3.x contract and DQX will derive quality rules from its schema, constraints, and explicit checks.

input.title: Contract source
input.description: Provide an ODCS v3.x contract in YAML or JSON. Schema validation requires every property to have a physicalType set to a valid Unity Catalog type.

options.predefinedHint: Derive is_not_null, is_unique, regex_match, range and length checks from property constraints.
options.schemaValidationHint: Emit a has_valid_schema rule per ODCS schema using each property's physicalType.
options.strictHint: Require exact column set, order, and types. Disable for permissive validation that allows extra columns.
options.textRulesHint: Convert the contract's text expectations (type: text) into checks using AI. Slower — each expectation is sent to the language model.
options.criticalityHint: Applied to predefined rules. Explicit rules keep the criticality declared in the contract.

rulesFromContract.generateHint: DQX parses the contract locally — no data is read from your workspace. Enable natural-language expectations to also convert the contract's free-text quality rules with AI.

errors.emptyContract: Paste or upload a contract first.
errors.generateFailed: Failed to generate rules from contract
errors.noTargets: Pick at least one target table before saving.
errors.missingColumns: Some target tables are missing columns referenced by the contract. Resolve the warnings before saving.

results.description: Review the generated rules, then map each ODCS schema to a Unity Catalog table to save them.
results.noSchemas: The contract didn't define any schemas, so nothing was generated.
results.saveSummary: Ready to save {{rules}} rule(s) across {{tables}} table(s).
results.pickTargets: Pick a target table for at least one schema to enable save.
results.fixColumnsHint: Resolve the column-mismatch warnings to enable save.
results.missingColumnsTitle_one: {{count}} target table is missing columns referenced by the contract
results.missingColumnsTitle_other: {{count}} target tables are missing columns referenced by the contract
results.missingColumnsBody: Saving is disabled until each contract field maps to a column on the chosen Unity Catalog table.
results.unassignedHint: These rules can't be auto-bucketed; they will be skipped unless the contract is updated.

schema.targetTableHint: Required. Rules will be saved as drafts against this table.
schema.noRules: No rules generated for this schema.
schema.missingTitle_one: {{count}} contract field is missing on the target table
schema.missingTitle_other: {{count}} contract fields are missing on the target table
schema.missingBody: These fields are referenced by the generated rules but do not exist on {{table}}:
schema.missingHint: Pick a different target table, or update the contract so its field names match the table.
schema.inspectErrorTitle: Could not list columns for the chosen table
schema.inspectErrorBody: Verify the table exists in Unity Catalog and that you have permission to read its metadata.

rulesImport.title: Import rules
rulesImport.subtitle: Import data quality rules from a YAML file.
rulesImport.subtitleYaml: Bulk-import rules from a DQX YAML file.
rulesImport.subtitleContract: Generate rules from an ODCS v3.x data contract — schema, constraints, and explicit DQX checks.
rulesImport.importDescription: Upload a YAML file or paste YAML content. Each entry should follow the DQX check format.
rulesImport.yamlMustBeList:
YAML must be a list of check definitions, for example:
- criticality: error
  check:
    function: is_not_null
    arguments:
      column: id
rulesImport.selectTargetTable: Select a target table to dry-run against
rulesImport.noBoundChecks: No table-bound checks to dry-run (cross-table SQL checks are skipped)
rulesImport.dryRunSubmitted: Dry run submitted — waiting for results...
rulesImport.failedFetchDryRun: Failed to fetch dry run results
rulesImport.failedCheckDryRunStatus: Failed to check dry run status
rulesImport.submitFailed_one: {{count}} rule failed to submit — saved as drafts instead
rulesImport.submitFailed_other: {{count}} rules failed to submit — saved as drafts instead
rulesImport.dryRunTooltip: Dry-run validates only the first 1–10,000 rows of the target table — it's a fast preview, not a full validation. To run imported rules against every row after approval, go to Run Rules and pick "All rows".

rulesCreateSql.title: Cross-table rules
rulesCreateSql.subtitle: Write SQL queries that validate data across tables or run dataset-level aggregation checks.
rulesCreateSql.generateWithAiHint: Describe the cross-table validation you need. Use fully qualified table names (catalog.schema.table) so the AI can write accurate SQL.
rulesCreateSql.generateWithAiPlaceholder: e.g. Check that every order in catalog.schema.orders has at least one line item in catalog.schema.line_items, and that the order total matches the sum of line item amounts
rulesCreateSql.duplicateTooltip: A SQL check with this name already exists. Remove or rename it.
rulesCreateSql.queryDescription: The query should return rows that violate the check (i.e., bad rows).
rulesCreateSql.nameLengthError: Name must be 128 characters or fewer.
rulesCreateSql.nameInvalidError: Name must start with a letter and contain only letters, numbers, and underscores.
rulesCreateSql.nameHint: Letters, numbers, and underscores only. Must start with a letter.
rulesCreateSql.querySemicolonError: Semicolons are not allowed. Enter a single SELECT query only.
rulesCreateSql.queryProhibitedError: Query contains prohibited keywords (DROP, DELETE, INSERT, etc.). Only SELECT queries are allowed.
rulesCreateSql.sqlQueryPlaceholder:
SELECT o.order_id, o.total, SUM(li.amount) AS line_total
FROM catalog.schema.orders o
JOIN catalog.schema.line_items li ON li.order_id = o.order_id
GROUP BY o.order_id, o.total
HAVING o.total != line_total
rulesCreateSql.sqlQueryHint: Use fully qualified table names (catalog.schema.table). Returned rows are treated as violations.
rulesCreateSql.everyCheckNeedsNameQuery: Every check needs a name and a SQL query
rulesCreateSql.checksAlreadyExist_one: {{count}} check already exists and cannot be saved
rulesCreateSql.checksAlreadyExist_other: {{count}} checks already exist and cannot be saved
rulesCreateSql.dryRunInProgressDescription: A dry run is currently running. Leaving will cancel it and discard any results.
rulesCreateSql.unsavedSqlChecks: You have unsaved SQL checks. Leaving will discard your changes.
rulesCreateSql.assignToTableOn: This rule will be grouped with the selected table's rules, making it available for that table's pipeline.
rulesCreateSql.assignToTableOff: Saved as a standalone cross-table check by default.
rulesCreateSql.enterSqlBeforeDryRun: Enter a SQL query before running a dry run
rulesCreateSql.dryRunSubmitted: Dry run submitted — waiting for results...
rulesCreateSql.notSqlRule: This rule isn't a cross-table SQL check, so it can't be edited here.
rulesCreateSql.noSqlGenerated: AI did not generate any SQL checks. Try being more specific about the tables and conditions.
rulesCreateSql.fetchResultsFailed: Dry run succeeded but failed to fetch results. Try refreshing.
rulesCreateSql.unknownDryRunError: Dry run failed with an unknown error.

rulesDrafts.title: Drafts & review
rulesDrafts.subtitle: Rule sets awaiting review, recently created drafts, and rejected sets.
rulesDrafts.orphanWarning_one: {{count}} rule missing an internal ID — actions are disabled until repaired.
rulesDrafts.orphanWarning_other: {{count}} rules missing an internal ID — actions are disabled until repaired.
rulesDrafts.noIdTooltip: This rule has no internal ID. Click "Repair IDs" above to fix.
rulesDrafts.submitTooltip: Send this rule for review and approval
rulesDrafts.cannotSubmitDuplicate: Cannot submit: same check exists as {{label}}
rulesDrafts.approveSingleBody: "{{name}}" will move to the active rule catalog and become eligible for runs.
rulesDrafts.rejectSingleBody: "{{name}}" will be marked rejected and removed from the approval queue. The author can revise and resubmit.
rulesDrafts.revokeTitle: Revoke submission and move back to draft
rulesDrafts.unrejectTitle: Move rejected rule back to draft
rulesDrafts.revokeFirstToEdit: Revoke this submission first to edit
rulesDrafts.authoredBy: Authored by {{user}} — only the author or an approver can edit, submit, or delete.
rulesDrafts.noDraftsAuthor: Newly created rules appear here for review before they become active.
rulesDrafts.deleteConfirmPrefix: Are you sure you want to delete this rule for
rulesDrafts.revokeBulkBody_one: Are you sure you want to revoke {{count}} selected rule? It will be moved back to draft status.
rulesDrafts.revokeBulkBody_other: Are you sure you want to revoke {{count}} selected rules? They will be moved back to draft status.
rulesDrafts.submitBulkBody: {{eligible}} of {{selected}} selected rule(s) will be submitted for approval.
rulesDrafts.submitBulkSkipped: {{count}} will be skipped (not eligible or duplicate).
rulesDrafts.approveBulkBody_one: Are you sure you want to approve {{count}} selected rule? Approved rules will become active immediately.
rulesDrafts.approveBulkBody_other: Are you sure you want to approve {{count}} selected rules? Approved rules will become active immediately.
rulesDrafts.rejectBulkBody_one: Are you sure you want to reject {{count}} selected rule? Rejected rules will be moved back to the drafts list.
rulesDrafts.rejectBulkBody_other: Are you sure you want to reject {{count}} selected rules? Rejected rules will be moved back to the drafts list.
rulesDrafts.deleteBulkBody_one: Are you sure you want to delete {{count}} selected rule? This action cannot be undone.
rulesDrafts.deleteBulkBody_other: Are you sure you want to delete {{count}} selected rules? This action cannot be undone.

rulesDrafts.bulkCannotAct: Cannot {{action}}: you can only act on rules you authored.

rulesSingleTable.title: Single table rules
rulesSingleTable.subtitle: Define column-level checks for an individual table.
rulesSingleTable.editSubtitle: Editing rules for {{table}}. Run a dry run or save changes.
rulesSingleTable.createSubtitle: Define rules with AI or manually, assign tables, then validate and save.
rulesSingleTable.validateSaveDescription: Run a dry run against a specific table to validate, then save rules.
rulesSingleTable.referenceTableLabel: Reference table (optional — provides column context to AI)
rulesSingleTable.aiPromptPlaceholder: e.g. All ID columns must not be null, email must be valid format, amounts must be positive
rulesSingleTable.sampleRowsTooltip: Dry-run validates only the first 1–10,000 rows of the selected table — it's a fast preview, not a full validation. To run rules against every row, go to Run Rules and pick "All rows".
rulesSingleTable.selectFunctionForEvery: Select a function for every check
rulesSingleTable.assignAtLeastOneTable: Assign at least one target table to a check

rulesSingleTable.unsavedChangesDescription: You have unsaved rule changes. Leaving will discard your edits.
rulesSingleTable.duplicateTooltip: This check already exists for the selected table(s). Remove or modify it.
rulesSingleTable.mutexUsingOther: Using "{{other}}" — clear it to use this field instead.
rulesSingleTable.mutexConflict: Provide either this or "{{other}}", not both.
rulesSingleTable.toastAllChecksHaveTarget: All checks already have a target table.
rulesSingleTable.toastAllChecksHaveTargetDescription: Use a check's "Target tables" panel to add {{table}} explicitly.
rulesSingleTable.toastTargetedChecksDescription: Checks with explicit targets were left unchanged.
rulesSingleTable.toastAiNoChecks: AI did not generate any valid checks. Try a different description.
rulesSingleTable.toastAiFailed: AI generation failed. Please try again.
rulesSingleTable.toastSelectTable: Select a table to run a dry run against
rulesSingleTable.toastNoChecksTargetTable: No checks target the selected table
rulesSingleTable.toastDryRunSubmitted: Dry run submitted — waiting for results...
rulesSingleTable.toastDryRunFetchFailed: Failed to fetch dry run results
rulesSingleTable.toastFailedCheckStatus: Failed to check dry run status
rulesSingleTable.toastOriginalTargetRemoved: {{table}}: original target was removed in the editor but the rule was kept. Delete it from Drafts if you no longer want it.
rulesSingleTable.toastDuplicatesExist:
Duplicate rules already exist and cannot be saved:
{{messages}}
rulesSingleTable.argInvalidColumnName: "{{name}}" is not a valid column name. Use letters, numbers, underscores, and dots only.
rulesSingleTable.argSemicolonsListNotAllowed: Semicolons are not allowed in list values.
rulesSingleTable.argSemicolonsExpressionNotAllowed: Semicolons are not allowed in expressions.
rulesSingleTable.argExpressionProhibitedKeyword: Expression contains a prohibited SQL keyword (DROP, DELETE, INSERT, etc.).
rulesSingleTable.crossArgRangeNeedsBound: Provide at least one of Min Limit or Max Limit.
rulesSingleTable.argHintIsUniqueColumns: comma-separated, e.g. id or col1, col2
rulesSingleTable.overrideSchemaColumnsHelp: Optional. Restrict the schema check to these columns; leave empty to validate the whole table against the expected schema.
rulesSingleTable.overrideHelpNullsDistinct: When true (default), NULL values are treated as distinct from each other.
rulesSingleTable.overrideHelpTrimStrings: When true, treat whitespace-only values as empty.
rulesSingleTable.overrideHelpCaseSensitive: When false, the comparison is case-insensitive.
rulesSingleTable.overrideHelpDateFormat: Optional. Leave blank to use DQX's default ISO date parser.
rulesSingleTable.overrideHelpTimestampFormat: Optional. Leave blank to use DQX's default ISO timestamp parser.
rulesSingleTable.overrideHelpNegateExpression: When true, the rule fails when the expression evaluates to true.
rulesSingleTable.overrideHelpNegateRegex: When true, the rule fails when the value matches the pattern.

runs.title: Run Rules
runs.subtitle: Run approved rules on demand or on a schedule. Results land in the validation history.
runs.executeRulesSubtitle: Select approved rules and trigger manual validation runs.
runs.noApprovedRulesDescription: Approve rules in Drafts & review first, then come back here to run them.

runs.yamlSyntaxOrValidation: Check YAML syntax or validation errors.
runs.noScopeMatched: No rule sets matched the schedule scope. Check that approved rules exist for the selected scope.
runs.noScheduledRunsDescription: Scheduled runs define how DQX Studio automatically processes your data quality checks on a recurring or time-based cadence.

roleManagement.title: Role Management
roleManagement.description: Map Databricks workspace groups to application roles. Users inherit the highest-priority role from their group memberships.
roleManagement.addMappingHint: Add a mapping below to assign roles to Databricks groups.
roleManagement.delayTitle: Role changes take up to ~1 minute to reach an active session.
roleManagement.delayBody: Each user's resolved role is cached client-side for 60 seconds. After a mapping is added or removed, the affected user keeps their previous role until their session revalidates (which also happens immediately on any page navigation). A hard refresh applies the new role straight away.
roleManagement.showingFirst: Showing first {{count}} matches — refine your search to narrow results.
roleManagement.roleAdminDescription: Full access including role management (admins are implicit runners)
roleManagement.roleApproverDescription: Can approve/reject rules and all author permissions
roleManagement.roleAuthorDescription: Can create, edit, and submit rules
roleManagement.roleRunnerDescription: Can run approved rules from the Run Rules page (additive — independent of other roles)
roleManagement.mappingAddedDescription: Stored in dq_role_mappings. Active sessions pick up the new role within ~1 minute (or on next page navigation).
roleManagement.mappingRemovedDescription: Affected users may keep their previous role for up to ~1 minute until their session refreshes.
roleManagement.fallbackError: Failed to create role mapping. Check the backend logs for details.
roleManagement.deleteMappingBody: Members of {{group}} will lose the {{role}} role. They may keep cached permissions for up to ~1 minute until their session refreshes.

rulesReview.yamlMustBeList: YAML must be a list of check definitions

rulesRegistry.title: Rules Registry
rulesRegistry.description: Browse, author, and govern reusable data quality rule definitions.

rulesRegistry.dialogDescription: Registry rules are reusable, table-agnostic templates. Apply them to a monitored table later to map slots to real columns.

rulesRegistry.nativeHint: Pick a check function to see its slots and parameters.

monitoredTables.title: Monitored Tables
monitoredTables.subtitle: Apply registry rules to tables and materialize them into active checks.
monitoredTables.emptyState: No monitored tables match these filters yet.
monitoredTables.deleteConfirmDescription: This removes "{{table}}" from Rules Registry governance and deletes every applied rule and materialized check tied to it. This can't be undone.
monitoredTables.registerDialogDescription: Pick a table to bring under Rules Registry governance. You'll map and apply rules to it next.
monitoredTables.stewardOptionalHint: Optional — the person responsible for this table's data quality.
monitoredTables.unpublishedChangesDescription: Applied rule changes are staged and won't run until you publish.
monitoredTables.neverPublishedDescription: This table has never been published. Publish to materialize its applied rules into active checks.
monitoredTables.noProfileHint: Run the profiler on this table from Profile & Generate to see column statistics here.
monitoredTables.emptyAppliedRules: No rules applied to this table yet.
monitoredTables.removeConfirmDescription: This deletes "{{name}}" from this table, including any materialized checks. This can't be undone.
monitoredTables.addRuleDialogDescription: Pick a published registry rule, then map its column slots to this table.
monitoredTables.noPublishedRules: No published rules match your search.
monitoredTables.createNewRuleHint: New rules start as drafts — submit and get them approved in Rules Registry before they can be applied.
monitoredTables.noMatchingColumns: No columns of this type on this table.
monitoredTables.mappingIncomplete: Map every slot to a column before applying.
monitoredTables.resultsDescription: Run history for checks materialized from this table's applied rules lives in Runs History.
monitoredTables.resultsTableFqnHint: Filter or search for "{{table}}" in Runs History to see its run results.
monitoredTables.notYetPublishedResultsHint: Publish this table to materialize its applied rules into checks before results appear here.
monitoredTables.invalidFqnHint: Select a table from the catalog browser.
monitoredTables.suggestRulesDialogDescription: Published rules ranked by relevance to this table's profile. Review each mapping before adding.
